jokes.text: There's no way a mouse could kill a scorpion. Dude, a mouse could kill... It's got sharp claws and teeth. A rat could kill a scorpion. No shit, Frank, but we're not talking about a rat.
jokes.setup: Characters arguing about whether a mouse could kill a scorpion
jokes.punchline: The pedantic distinction between mouse and rat in a completely pointless argument
jokes.explanation: Establishes the gang's tendency to argue about meaningless topics with intense passion. Good character work.

jokes.text: Shut up! Shut up! Oh, my God! I don't care! All we're doing is arguing about the most stupid shit
jokes.punchline: Charlie's meta-commentary on their own stupidity
jokes.explanation: Charlie as the occasional voice of reason is always funny. Good economy in the delivery.

jokes.text: It's goddamn bright out here. It's so bright. I can't use my eyes. There's people playing football right next to us. Like there's no room to drink.
jokes.setup: Characters going outside for fresh air to improve their mood
jokes.punchline: Immediate complaints about the outdoors and concern about drinking space
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - these terrible people can't handle basic outdoor activities. 'No room to drink' is a great punchline.

jokes.text: Oh! Was that the one where the guy from... the New Kids on the Block makes it onto the Eagles? I saw that.
jokes.punchline: Confusing Mark Wahlberg with his brother from New Kids on the Block
jokes.explanation: Good character ignorance moment. Wahlberg confusion is relatable and specific.

jokes.text: No, no, no, no, no. You... You... You're a woman. Women can't do things like this. You'll get hurt.
jokes.setup: Dee wanting to join the football tryout competition
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate sexist dismissal with fake concern
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac casual sexism. The stammering delivery adds to the cringe.

jokes.text: Yeah. Like, uh, cooking and... Complaining to your friends about your boyfriends. Yeah. Playing... Playing at ballet. Cleaning. Displaying cars at auto shows in tiny bikinis.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous and sexist suggestions ending with car shows
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure. Each suggestion gets more ridiculous and offensive.

jokes.text: Well, in high school, Charlie was like our school mascot. A mascot nobody wanted. He'd get wasted and dress in this green spandex bodysuit.
jokes.punchline: A mascot nobody wanted
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Charlie's pathetic desperation. 'A mascot nobody wanted' is a great line with excellent economy.

jokes.text: Green Man was good. It got me through some hard times. But I'm done with it.
jokes.punchline: The pathetic admission that a mascot costume 'got him through hard times'
jokes.explanation: Charlie's earnest delivery of this pathetic confession is peak character comedy

jokes.text: Are you planning on getting yourself locked in the bathroom of your cousin's Winnebago for three days? Shut up about that. I survived on hand soap and toilet water for three days. The memory haunts me.
jokes.punchline: Frank's overly dramatic response about surviving on hand soap and toilet water
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Frank's bizarre past. The haunted delivery sells it.

jokes.text: I've seen a lot of people wearing capes. There are, like, 15 guys wearing capes. Why would you wear a cape? What advantage does that give you? Absolutely none.
jokes.punchline: Logical analysis of cape inefficiency at football tryouts
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about delusional wannabe athletes. Dennis applying logic to absurdity.

jokes.text: You done pissing yet? No. No. I haven't even started pissing because you're standing next to me, you're talking to me. It's making me nervous, and I can't do my thing.
jokes.setup: Mac asking if Dennis is done urinating
jokes.punchline: Dennis's stage fright preventing him from starting
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis anxiety moment. Relatable awkwardness executed well.

jokes.text: Want to drop some acid with me? What? You're dropping acid? Mmm. Woodstock, baby.
jokes.punchline: Woodstock justification for inappropriate drug use
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - casual drug offer with delusional reasoning. Good setup for later chaos.

jokes.text: No. You messed up. It's too high. It doesn't look right on you. Too high? No, I'm good at it, dude. I did a good job on you. That's 'cause I have good cheekbones.
jokes.punchline: Dennis's vanity about his cheekbones during makeup application
jokes.explanation: Dennis's vanity shining through even during sports preparation is classic character work

jokes.text: What the hell is this? How's it hangin'? This is a joke, right? You think anyone's gonna buy that?
jokes.setup: Dee appearing in drag to try out as a man
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate dismissal of her terrible disguise
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy gold. Dee's desperation and Mac's blunt assessment work well together.

jokes.text: Dee, the second any part of your body touches that field, it's gonna shatter like glass. You got bones like glass. You spent half your life in a back brace. Your body's, like, 90% scoliosis, Dee.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and cruel medical assessments
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis cruelty with medical specificity. '90% scoliosis' is a great exaggeration.

jokes.text: You think you dipshits are gonna play on the same field that champions play on? Where are we going? We're going to a shitty high school in Bucks County.
jokes.setup: Characters excited about playing at Lincoln Field
jokes.punchline: Reality of going to a crappy high school field
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection joke. Deflates their expectations perfectly with brutal efficiency.

jokes.text: Stay here and look at the Linc? Like a couple of dicks in a yard?
jokes.punchline: Mac's crude but accurate assessment of their situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac line. Crude but poetic in its own way. Great economy.

jokes.text: I like your mustache. Oh. Thank you. Hmm. I can't grow one. Okay. That's a pretty sweet cape. Thank you. My mom made it. Hmm. She did a very good job. Yeah. She's talented.
jokes.punchline: The revelation that the grown man's mom made his cape
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. The mom-made cape detail perfectly captures the wannabe athlete delusion.

jokes.text: When I'm done with this tryout you'll go back to your sad, pitiful life and I'll be on the Eagles. And everybody in Philadelphia will know the name McPoyle.
jokes.punchline: The irony of a McPoyle calling someone else's life sad and pitiful
jokes.explanation: Perfect McPoyle delusion. The lack of self-awareness is pitch-perfect for the character.

jokes.text: The unibrow. Yeah. The eczema. The acne. He's definitely a McPoyle. There are so many of those people.
jokes.punchline: Clinical listing of disgusting physical traits and casual acceptance of their prevalence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and world-building. Treats McPoyles like a species to be studied.

jokes.text: Classic mistake, Frank. You're making a classic mistake, bro. Just shut up and sit in your stupid chair. It's your funeral, buddy.
jokes.punchline: Mac's ominous warning about double-dosing followed by immediate dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good foreshadowing of Frank's upcoming chaos. Sets up the payoff.

jokes.text: The McPoyle brothers. Hello, Charles. What the hell are you guys doing here? We came to support our brother Doyle. He's gonna make the Eagles.
jokes.punchline: Their sincere belief that Doyle will make the Eagles
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment. McPoyle delusion is consistent and funny.

jokes.text: What is that a jar of? Pig parts. How many can there possibly be? Oh, good to see her again. Good. She doesn't know what I'm saying, right?
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of pig parts and concern about the mute sister understanding
jokes.explanation: Good McPoyle weirdness. Pig parts jar is perfectly disturbing and random.

jokes.text: They've got thin corneas. What is that? A fife? You brought your little flute.
jokes.punchline: Random medical condition and bringing a fife to football tryouts
jokes.explanation: Peak McPoyle absurdity. Thin corneas and a fife are perfect random details.

jokes.text: We're doing these tryouts for you because of your harassment and your love for the New Kids on the Block movie.
jokes.punchline: Calling it harassment and referencing the earlier movie confusion
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier confusion. Coach's bluntness lands well.

jokes.text: Donovan McNabb. Hey, guys. I'm Donovan McNabb. Uh, I play quarterback for the Philadelphia Eagles. And I'm here to tell you that you can, too if you start every day with a hearty breakfast from McDonald's.
jokes.setup: Anticipation of motivational speech from Eagles quarterback
jokes.punchline: Immediate pivot to McDonald's commercial
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection. Subverts expectations perfectly with corporate sponsorship absurdity.

jokes.text: Hey, guys. I'm Donovan McNabb. And I'm here to tell you that you can, too... if you start every day with a hearty breakfast from McDonald's.
jokes.setup: Supposed motivational speech from NFL star
jokes.punchline: Turns into obvious McDonald's commercial
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that subverts expectations with corporate sponsorship absurdity

jokes.text: That was not Donovan McNabb! That was not Donovan McNabb! Yes, it was. It was like a McDonald's plug. Was that the guy from The Cosby Show? That's not the guy from... Yeah. He was married to Sondra. Alvin. Alvin.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly wrong identifications ending with a random name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of confusion. Each wrong guess gets more specific and ridiculous.

jokes.text: I did not take any acid. Remember? Oh, yeah, you did. What are you talking about? I put a shitload of it in your beer. What? Yeah. Is that what all those little pieces of paper were floating... I drank all that shit, dude!
jokes.setup: Charlie thinking he didn't take acid
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Frank dosed his beer without telling him
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection payoff. Frank's casual drugging is peak terrible person behavior.

jokes.text: That's okay. There was, like, a ton of acid in there! Yeah. Why would you do that? I don't want to be the only one tripping.
jokes.setup: Charlie panicking about accidental acid consumption
jokes.punchline: Frank's selfish reasoning for drugging him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank logic. Completely selfish reasoning delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: Oh, no, he didn't! Oh, no, he didn't! That's my dance. That's my signature dance. Yeah. I'm gonna do that when I tackle people.
jokes.setup: Mac seeing someone do a dance
jokes.punchline: Claiming ownership of a generic celebration dance
jokes.explanation: Good Mac delusion. His ownership of common moves is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Ah. Uh, hitting's not really my thing, Coach. Spoken like a true champion. You just might make the team. Awesome. Really? Jesus Christ.
jokes.punchline: Coach's sarcastic praise being taken literally
jokes.explanation: Great irony joke. Dennis's cluelessness about sarcasm is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Oh! Crap! I'm starting to feel weird. What the hell? Oh, no. Not again. Oh, no! Hey! Hey! Hey! Hey! Somebody get me outta here!
jokes.punchline: Callback to his Woodstock bathroom trauma
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback execution. Frank's PTSD is both funny and character-specific.

jokes.text: What is going on with his head? It's too small or tight or something. Yeah, that's it. His skin's too tight. My skin's not tight.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre observation about tight skin and defensive response
jokes.explanation: Great acid-induced observation. 'Skin's too tight' is a memorably weird line.

jokes.text: What is going on with his head? It's too small or tight or something. Yeah, that's it. His skin's too tight.
jokes.punchline: His bizarre fixation on someone having 'too tight' skin
jokes.explanation: Charlie's acid-fueled observations are wonderfully bizarre and quotable

jokes.text: My form is perfect. I'm like Jerry Rice. Feel that stride. So fluid and fast. I've got the stride of a gazelle. A beautiful, beautiful gazelle person.
jokes.setup: Dennis's internal monologue during football drill
jokes.punchline: Increasingly narcissistic self-assessment culminating in 'gazelle person'
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis narcissism. 'Beautiful, beautiful gazelle person' is an all-time great line with perfect escalation.

jokes.text: All right, Dee. You can do this. You can kick a football. You're better at sports than Mac and Dennis.
jokes.punchline: The low bar of being better than Mac and Dennis
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Sets up her surprising competence with low expectations.

jokes.text: No. No. Those storklike legs... they act like pendulums. And on the bottoms of those pendulums, feet like wrecking balls.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain Dee's surprising athletic ability
jokes.punchline: Comparing her legs to construction equipment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor. Dennis's scientific analysis of insults is peak character work.

jokes.text: I'm Artemis. I'm Dee's friend. I'm trapped. I know. But I can help. Crawl into my mouth.
jokes.punchline: Artemis offering to help by having him crawl into her mouth
jokes.explanation: Peak acid trip absurdity. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it even weirder.

jokes.text: You know why? Because you are the Green Man. Green Man is saving your life right now, bro. Just go with the flow.
jokes.setup: Charlie on acid dealing with McPoyles
jokes.punchline: Green Man as psychological protection
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Green Man origin. Charlie's acid logic is endearing.

jokes.text: How'd you get in here? What are you talking about? I've been stuck in this bathroom for three hours. I think he took a dump in there.
jokes.punchline: Mac's casual assessment that he defecated in the portable toilet
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out callback. Mac's matter-of-fact delivery works well.

jokes.text: Lizard, am I standing in poop? I'm about to demonstrate to all of you my natural abilities as a punter.
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to Dee's serious athletic declaration
jokes.explanation: Great juxtaposition between Frank's chaos and Dee's determination. Good editing joke.

jokes.text: Oh! Oh, I broke it. Oh, it may be broken. Oh, sweet Jesus, I think I broke it.
jokes.setup: Dee attempting to prove women can play football
jokes.punchline: Immediate catastrophic injury
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff structure. Dee's competence immediately undermined by injury.

jokes.text: So your bones are made out of glass. Yes! Nice. No, my bones aren't... Know what? It doesn't matter, because I made the furthest, so I win.
jokes.setup: Dee's injury proving Dennis's earlier assessment
jokes.punchline: Dee's stubborn insistence she still won despite catastrophic failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Dennis's prediction. Dee's denial in the face of evidence is classic character work.

jokes.text: Holy shit. This thing is loaded. My leg! Doyle, are you okay? My leg! Somebody shot my leg! No!
jokes.punchline: Shooting the one person who might actually make the team
jokes.explanation: Great escalation to chaos. Shooting Doyle is the perfect ironic conclusion to their scheme.

jokes.text: Oh, that's not good. That's not good. No. That could come back and bite us in the ass. That's not a good thing.
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of the severity
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement humor. Their casual concern about consequences is perfectly in character.
